content_preview.title: RedAccess Finds More Than 5,000 Vibe-Coded Apps on Lovable, Replit, Base44 and Netlify Running With No Authentication
content_preview.summary: Israeli security firm RedAccess says vibe-coding platforms ship apps that default to public, exposing medical, financial and corporate data to anyone who can find them.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A scan published this week by the Israeli cybersecurity firm RedAccess found more than 5,000 applications built on AI vibe-coding platforms running with virtually no security or authentication, with roughly 40 percent of those apps exposing sensitive corporate and personal data to anyone on the open web, according to [Slashdot](https://yro.slashdot.org/story/26/05/08/1731257/thousands-of-vibe-coded-apps-expose-corporate-and-personal-data-on-the-open-web). The platforms named in the ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong 637-word security exposé. Clear separation of RedAccess findings, platform responses, methodology dispute, and explicit unknown-fraction caveats.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 snapshots. Slashdot (source-0) confirms verbatim Dor Zvi quotes 'organizations are actually leaking private data through vibe-coding applications' + 'one of the biggest events ever where people are exposing corporate or other sensitive information to anyone in the world' + 'anyone from your company at any moment can generate an app, and this is not going through any development cycle or any security check'. Lovable phishing-impersonation list (Bank of America, Costco, FedEx, Trader Joe's, McDonald's) — verified verbatim. Axios (source-1, Archive.org fallback) confirms 380,000 baseline scan, 5,000 sensitive-data figure, Replit/Masad '24 hours' notice, default-public configuration, Brazilian bank + U.K. cabinet supplier + UK clinical trials + shipping vessel examples. VentureBeat (source-2) confirms ~5,000 figure cross-reference.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (figures, platform names, brand impersonation list, executive name) traces to a verifiable cited source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Properly gives space to platform responses (Masad, Base44/Lovable spokespeople) without endorsing either side.
editor_notes.originality: No archive collision. New disclosure event.
concerns: yro.slashdot.org not in config/source_allowlist.txt. Slashdot is a long-established tech-news aggregator and the primary outlet for the Zvi quotes; content verified verbatim against snapshot.
Axios fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200 but failed snapshot capture). Editorial-process metadata, not a factual issue.
recommendations: Add yro.slashdot.org or slashdot.org to allowlist — it surfaces tech-press coverage with verifiable content.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong piece. Two recoverable editorial-process issues (allowlist gap + Archive.org fallback) covered by single corrections record.
